# The case for pre lecture videos
There are positives and negatives of the idea in general. One major positive is the popular support among students, this is shown in the graphical analysis.
Students could use this material to review lessons for recalling information, or find tough spots to review.
Instructors could begin with a small base of knowledge in the class so they can focus attention on more significant issues.
<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->


## Counterpoints
This could take away from the understanding of those who are unable to access or undertand the pre lecture material, or watse time if the pre lecture material is gone over again in class.
This material could also be difficult to create for instructors, and could limit engagement by decreasing attendance of in person class.

## Graphical analysis
<img src="siteproject/static/imgs/linechart" alt="Linechart representing the response to the survey. "  width="500"/>
<img src="siteproject/static/imgs/barchart.png" alt="Barchart representing response to the survey "  width="500"/>
<img src="siteproject/static/imgs/distribution.png" alt="Graphed distribution of percentages of selctions "  width="500"/>

## conclusion

In conclusion, it could help the high acheivers and those lagging behind but putting in effort, but could cause many people to not attend class and fall behind. However, the class overall supports the idea, so with the addition of a possible small attendance penalty, I support it's adoption.
